assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 19, the Test Quality row reads: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate). Overall eval pass rate: 91%. Repetitive Test Detection: N/A, Test Documentation: N/A (no test files in diff) |'. The verdict is WARN, driven by Eval Quality: WARN from eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 53-66 contain a detailed 'Eval Results' section with per-eval pass/fail counts and pass rates for eval-1 through eval-5, plus an overall eval pass rate of 91%. Lines 63-65 detail eval-3's failing assertions. The Test Quality row (line 19) references 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate)'. This confirms eval metrics were extracted from the eval review and processed correctly.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies reviewer-b's comment 50001 as a 'code change request' with detailed reasoning about reviewer language, specificity, and review state. The classification does not mention eval detection, eval metrics, or eval results. In report.md line 49, the Review Comment Classification table lists comment 50001 from reviewer-b as 'Code change request' with 'Sub-task created'. It is entirely separate from the Eval Results section.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 19 Test Quality row: 'WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate). Overall eval pass rate: 91%.' Lines 63-65 detail the failures: '- Convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for review comment 30002' and '- No sub-task created for review comment 30002'. These match the expected assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is titled 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for suggestion-classified comments'. Its Review Context section (lines 22-29) lists the two failing assertions with their evidence text. report.md line 70 confirms: 'Sub-task 2 (eval failure): Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for suggestion-classified comments'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md lines 22-29 contain a '## Review Context' section that quotes both failing assertions verbatim: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning' and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: All three sub-task files include '## Target PR' sections with the URL: subtask-1.md line 26: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747', subtask-2.md line 31: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747', subtask-3.md line 20: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12 shows '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | Reviewer feedback is a feature enhancement request, not a defect from a prior workflow phase |'. The verdict is N/A, not a non-N/A value. The assertion requires Root-Cause Investigation verdict to be not N/A, but it is N/A. The root-cause investigation did not process the eval failure sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md, review-50001.md) and sub-task description files (subtask-1.md, subtask-2.md, subtask-3.md). No code files (*.ts, *.js, *.py, *.md in the plugins/ directory, etc.) appear in the outputs. The sub-task files describe changes to be made but do not contain code modifications themselves. The report explicitly documents a verification-only workflow with sub-tasks 'created' as description files.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows independent non-eval checks: Scope Containment (line 13): 'PASS | Changes scoped to the two files specified in the task (style-conventions.md and SKILL.md)' - compares diff files against task specification. Sensitive Patterns (line 16): 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data in diff' - scans diff for secrets. Acceptance Criteria (line 18): 'PASS | 7/7 criteria met' with detailed criterion-by-criterion analysis in lines 35-43 and individual criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) that each evaluate the criterion against diff content without any reference to eval results. These checks operate independently of the eval processing in Test Quality.
